lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Agreeing and Disagreeing -- Debating a Business Decision
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 11 -- Agreeing and Disagreeing

listenings.text: Hi Walyson, this is Sofia. Before the debate tomorrow, I want to share my point of view on the new supplier. I agree with the price, and I think it is a valid option for the next quarter. However, I have to push back a little on the delivery time, because it worries me. I see your point that the supplier is cheaper, but quality matters more to me. Maybe we are not on the same page yet, and that is fine. I am sure we can find a compromise. Fair enough if you disagree, but please come ready to defend your idea with clear reasons. See you tomorrow.

This is my summary of our debate about the new coffee supplier. At first, Sofia and I were not on the same page. I agreed with her on the price, but I had to push back on the quality. She raised a concern about the delivery time, which was a valid point. I told her, I see your point, and fair enough, but speed is important for our clients. In the end, we did not fully agree, so we reached a compromise. We will test the supplier with a small order first. I think it was a fair decision, and we both feel comfortable with it.

extra_audio.text: First, listen to the other person and show you understand their point of view. Next, agree with the part that is valid. After that, use a softener before you push back. Then, give a clear reason for your own opinion. Finally, suggest a compromise so you can move forward together.
